SELLER_SERVICER.definition: A Freddie Mac-approved counterparty that sells mortgages to Freddie Mac and/or services Freddie Mac-owned mortgages under the Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide and related agreements.
examples: Approved multifamily Seller/Servicer #12345 with delegated underwriting authority
Non-delegated Seller/Servicer using Freddie Mac underwriting review
Seller/Servicer under heightened monitoring due to QC findings
business_rules: Seller/Servicers must comply with the Freddie Mac Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide and applicable Bulletins with specific effective dates.
Delegated authorities (e.g., underwriting/servicing) constrain what actions a Seller/Servicer may take without Freddie Mac approval.
Seller/Servicers must maintain operational capacity and financial condition consistent with Freddie Mac eligibility requirements.

FREDDIE_MAC.definition: The government-sponsored enterprise that purchases, securitizes, and oversees servicing of multifamily mortgages in accordance with its Guide, Bulletins, and program requirements.
examples: Freddie Mac Multifamily purchase of a delivered mortgage
Freddie Mac issuance of Bulletin M2026-2 dated 04/21/26
Freddie Mac securitization into a Participation Certificate (PC)
business_rules: Freddie Mac establishes Guide requirements and enforces compliance through delivery reviews, QC, and servicing oversight.
Bulletins introduce changes with effective dates; impacted transactions must apply the correct version by date and applicability.
Freddie Mac may require corrective action or repurchase/remedies for noncompliance.

BORROWER.definition: The obligor(s) responsible for repayment under a multifamily mortgage and for making payments that are remitted through the Servicer to Freddie Mac or security holders.
examples: Single-asset entity (SAE) borrower owning a 150-unit property
Limited partnership borrower with a managing member
Borrower under a loan modification during servicing
business_rules: Borrower eligibility and credit profile affect underwriting decisions and ongoing servicing risk assessments.
Borrower payment performance drives delinquency status and default management actions.
Borrower communications (e.g., adverse action, default notices) must satisfy legal and Guide requirements.

LOAN_APPLICATION.definition: The borrower’s formal request for credit for a mortgage, including financial, property, and sponsorship information used for underwriting and approval decisions.
examples: New acquisition loan application for a multifamily property
Refinance loan application submitted via a delegated Seller/Servicer
Loan application declined triggering an adverse action notice
business_rules: Incomplete applications may not be underwritten or delivered until required third-party reports and borrower information are obtained.
Approval/denial decisions must be supported by documented underwriting rationale and required notices.
Application data must be consistent with delivered loan data used for purchase and securitization.

MORTGAGE.definition: A multifamily real estate loan (including note and security instrument) that may be originated, delivered to Freddie Mac, serviced, and potentially securitized into PCs.
examples: Fixed-rate multifamily mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac
Floating-rate mortgage held in a warehouse line prior to delivery
Mortgage pooled into a 55-Day Multi PC securitization
business_rules: Delivered mortgages must satisfy Freddie Mac underwriting, documentation, title/insurance, and delivery requirements.
Mortgage terms and servicing parameters must align with the remittance and reporting rules applicable to the product/PC structure.
Noncompliance can trigger purchase denial, delivery conditions, repurchase, or servicing remedies.

PROMISSORY_NOTE.definition: The executed debt instrument evidencing the borrower’s obligation to repay the mortgage under specified terms.
examples: Executed note with fixed interest rate and 30-year amortization
Modified note executed as part of a loan modification agreement
Note endorsed to Freddie Mac upon delivery
business_rules: The note must be properly executed and endorsed/assigned as required for delivery and enforceability.
Note terms must match delivered data and servicing setup to avoid reporting and remittance errors.
Any modification must be documented and, where required, approved and reported per Guide requirements.

SECURITY_INSTRUMENT.definition: The mortgage/deed of trust/security deed granting a lien on the property as collateral for the mortgage obligation.
examples: Deed of trust recorded in county land records
Mortgage security instrument with first-lien position
Security instrument amended via recorded modification
business_rules: Recording and lien perfection must satisfy delivery requirements and title insurance coverage expectations.
Lien position and permitted exceptions are constrained by program and Guide requirements.
Recorded instrument data must align with the title policy and closing documentation.

MULTIFAMILY_PROPERTY.definition: The income-producing residential real estate collateral securing a multifamily mortgage, including physical characteristics and legal/ownership attributes.
examples: 200-unit garden-style apartment community in Texas
Mixed-use property with residential units above retail space
Affordable housing property with regulatory restrictions
business_rules: Property eligibility and risk attributes drive underwriting standards and third-party report requirements.
Collateral must be properly insured and covered by acceptable title insurance with required endorsements/exceptions.
Changes to property condition, occupancy, or ownership can trigger servicing reporting and covenant compliance actions.

APPRAISAL_REPORT.definition: A valuation report prepared by a qualified appraiser to estimate the market value and/or other collateral metrics used in underwriting and delivery.
examples: MAI appraisal supporting a $35M value conclusion
Update appraisal required due to aging report date at closing
Appraisal with extraordinary assumptions disclosed
business_rules: Appraisal must meet scope, recency, and independence requirements applicable to the transaction and product.
Appraised value and assumptions must be consistent with underwriting conclusions and loan sizing.
Material appraisal deficiencies can block approval, delivery, or require remediation and re-review.

CREDIT_REPORT.definition: A third-party report summarizing credit history and obligations for borrower principals/guarantors as required for underwriting and risk assessment.
examples: Tri-merge credit report for key principals
Business credit report for borrowing entity
Credit report showing recent derogatory items
business_rules: Credit reports must be current enough for underwriting and reflect the correct parties per program requirements.
Material adverse findings require documented evaluation and may affect eligibility/terms or trigger decline.
Credit report data used in underwriting must reconcile to application and delivered loan file documentation.

TITLE_INSURANCE_POLICY.definition: A title insurance policy insuring the lien priority and marketability of title subject to allowed exceptions and endorsements required for Freddie Mac delivery.
examples: ALTA loan policy issued with required endorsements
Title policy with specific permitted easement exceptions
Title policy requiring curative action for a recorded lien
business_rules: Title insurance must meet minimum coverage, insured party, and endorsement requirements for delivery.
Unpermitted exceptions or defects must be cured or otherwise addressed per Guide before delivery.
Policy amount and insured lien position must align with the mortgage and recording details.

HAZARD_INSURANCE_POLICY.definition: Property insurance coverage protecting the collateral against specified hazards and satisfying Freddie Mac insurance requirements for the mortgage.
examples: Property hazard policy with Freddie Mac named as mortgagee/loss payee
Insurance renewal evidence provided prior to policy expiration
Policy change increasing deductible beyond permitted limits
business_rules: Minimum coverage types/amounts and maximum deductible limits must be satisfied throughout the loan term.
Lapses or insufficient coverage trigger servicing actions and may constitute an event of default.
Policy endorsements and loss payee language must align with Guide requirements and closing instructions.

CLOSING_PACKAGE.definition: The executed set of closing deliverables (loan documents and required certificates/evidence) supporting enforceability, delivery, and file retention requirements.
examples: Complete closing package including recorded security instrument and final title policy
Closing package pending receipt of updated insurance certificate
Digitally stored closing package in an approved eVault
business_rules: All required executed and recorded documents must be present and compliant before final delivery to Freddie Mac.
Defects (missing signatures, incorrect vesting, recording issues) require cure within specified timelines.
File retention must satisfy Guide retention periods and audit accessibility requirements.

LOAN_DELIVERY.definition: The process and event of submitting an originated mortgage and required data/documents to Freddie Mac for purchase and/or securitization eligibility.
examples: Final delivery accepted after satisfaction of title insurance conditions
Delivery suspended pending third-party report update
Delivery rejected due to data/document mismatch
business_rules: Delivery requires complete and accurate data and documentation per Guide chapters for documentation and deliveries.
Outstanding delivery conditions must be resolved before purchase/settlement or within permitted post-closing timeframes.
Delivered loan attributes must match legal documents and third-party reports to avoid purchase and securitization defects.

WAREHOUSING.definition: The interim holding period/process where a mortgage is funded and held prior to final delivery/purchase, often under a warehouse line with controls for collateral and documentation.
examples: Loan held in warehouse pending final title policy receipt
Bailee letter executed for note custody
Warehouse release after Freddie Mac settlement
business_rules: Custody and control of original notes and collateral documents must be maintained to preserve enforceability and delivery integrity.
Warehousing timelines and documentation controls must prevent stale reports and post-closing defects.
Warehouse release must align with settlement/purchase and custodial transfer requirements.

SERVICER.definition: The party responsible for collecting borrower payments, administering escrow/insurance, managing defaults, and performing accounting and reporting to Freddie Mac.
examples: Seller/Servicer acting as Servicer for Freddie Mac-owned loans
Subservicer performing collections and reporting under oversight
Servicer implementing a remediation plan after reporting errors
business_rules: Servicers must report and remit on a monthly basis using the correct net yield method and product rules.
Servicers must track and report delinquency status accurately and timely for Active/Delinquent/Deactivated classifications.
Servicing actions (e.g., modifications, defaults) may require approvals and must be documented and reported per Guide.

SERVICING_REPORT.definition: The periodic reporting submitted by the Servicer covering loan status, payment activity, delinquency, and other servicing metrics required by Freddie Mac.
examples: Monthly servicing report indicating 30 days delinquent status
Servicing report reflecting partial payment and suspense handling
Corrected servicing report resubmitted after reconciliation
business_rules: Reports must be submitted on schedule and reconcile to remittances and servicing system records.
Delinquency and status classifications must follow defined terms (e.g., Active Mortgage, deactivation for accounting/reporting).
Material reporting errors require correction, root-cause analysis, and may trigger QC findings.

ACCOUNTING_REPORT.definition: The Servicer’s accounting and remittance-related reporting to Freddie Mac, including interest calculations, net yield reporting, and reconciliation items.
examples: Monthly accounting report where accounting net yield differs due to purchase premium
Reconciliation report identifying remittance variance
Exception report for incorrect net yield setup
business_rules: Accounting Net Yield must be reported and used for remittances consistent with Required Net Yield rules, adjusted for premium/discount where applicable.
Monthly remittance and reporting must reconcile; variances require timely correction and explanation.
Reporting logic must align with product type (e.g., PC structures with payment delay) and loan status.

MORTGAGE_STATUS.definition: A classification of a mortgage for servicing, accounting, and reporting purposes, including terms such as Active Mortgage, Delinquent Mortgage, and deactivation status.
examples: ACTIVE status where borrower payments are current
DELINQUENT status at 30 days delinquent
DEACTIVATED for accounting/reporting after specified servicing action
business_rules: Status must be determined consistently using defined delinquency measures and Guide definitions.
Deactivation for accounting/reporting must meet criteria and be tracked with reasons and effective dates.
Status drives downstream actions: default management workflow, reporting requirements, and potentially securitization cashflow treatment.

DELINQUENCY.definition: A servicing measure indicating how late a borrower is on required payments, commonly expressed in days delinquent (e.g., 30 days delinquent).
examples: 30 days delinquent classification as of month-end
60 days delinquent with two installments past due
Cured delinquency after full reinstatement payment
business_rules: Days delinquent must be calculated consistently and drive correct status and reporting classification.
Delinquency buckets trigger escalation timelines, notices, and default management actions.
Delinquency reporting must reconcile to borrower payment posting and suspense rules.

PARTICIPATION_CERTIFICATE.definition: A securitization instrument (PC) representing interests in pooled mortgages, where borrower payments are passed through to PC holders under defined delay and remittance structures.
examples: 55-Day Multi PC with 55-day payment delay
Standard PC backed by a defined pool of multifamily mortgages
PC tranche issued from a securitization transaction
business_rules: PC type and payment delay determine timing of cashflow pass-through and servicing remittance obligations.
Only eligible mortgages meeting pooling criteria may be securitized into a given PC type.
Servicer reporting and remittance must align with PC structure to prevent investor distribution errors.

MORTGAGE_POOL.definition: A defined group of securitized mortgages aggregated for issuance of a Participation Certificate (PC), subject to pooling eligibility criteria and servicing/remittance rules.
examples: Pool of 55-Day Multi PC Securitized Mortgages
Pool assembled from multiple delivered mortgages in a month
Pool with weighted average coupon reported to investors
business_rules: Pooling criteria restrict which mortgages can be included based on product features and compliance status.
Pool metrics must be computed from loan-level data and reconcile to issuance disclosures.
Servicer of record must support pooling cashflow rules and reporting timelines.

ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD.definition: The net yield rate used by the Servicer to report and remit interest to Freddie Mac on a monthly basis; it equals Required Net Yield except when mortgages were sold at a discount or premium.
examples: Accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase
Accounting net yield adjusted due to premium purchase price
Accounting net yield adjusted due to discount purchase price
business_rules: Accounting Net Yield must be set and maintained accurately to drive correct monthly interest remittance.
When a mortgage is sold at premium/discount, Accounting Net Yield deviates from Required Net Yield as defined by Guide accounting rules.
Net yield setup errors require correction and may require reconciliations and make-whole adjustments.

REQUIRED_NET_YIELD.definition: The baseline net yield rate required under Freddie Mac remittance rules; used as a reference point for Accounting Net Yield reporting and remittances.
examples: Required net yield for a fixed-rate mortgage remittance type
Required net yield for a specific PC program
Required net yield updated due to contract amendment
business_rules: Required Net Yield is determined by product/remittance terms and must be applied consistently to eligible loans.
Servicer must validate Required Net Yield inputs and changes against program/contract references.
Differences between Required Net Yield and Accounting Net Yield must be explainable under premium/discount rules.

ADVERSE_ACTION_NOTICE.definition: An oral or written communication informing a borrower of refusal to grant an application for an extension of credit, including information required by applicable law.
examples: Written adverse action notice sent after application denial
Oral adverse action notice followed by required written disclosures
Adverse action notice citing insufficient credit history and DSCR
business_rules: If an application is refused, the adverse action notice must include all legally required content and be delivered within applicable timelines.
Notice content must align with underwriting decision rationale and file documentation.
Notices must be retained for audit and compliance review according to retention requirements.

FILE_RETENTION_POLICY.definition: The requirements defining how long and in what form loan origination, delivery, and servicing records must be retained and made available for review.
examples: Retention policy for closing package originals vs. electronic copies
Retention policy for servicing reports and remittance reconciliations
Legal hold applied to a loan file under dispute
business_rules: Loan files must be retained for the required period and be retrievable for Freddie Mac reviews, audits, and regulatory requests.
Electronic storage must preserve integrity, authenticity, and access controls for sensitive records.
Destruction must be suspended under legal hold or active investigation requirements.

THIRD_PARTY_REPORT.definition: An externally produced report used in underwriting, delivery, or servicing (e.g., appraisal, credit, engineering, environmental), subject to currency and quality requirements.
examples: Environmental site assessment report used in underwriting
Property condition assessment report required for delivery
Engineering report triggering repair escrow requirements
business_rules: Third-party reports must meet eligibility, independence, and recency requirements for the applicable loan product and stage.
Material findings must be evaluated and may require conditions, reserves, repairs, or denial.
Reports must be retained and traceable to underwriting decisions and delivered loan terms.

QUALITY_CONTROL_REVIEW.definition: A formal review process assessing loan file completeness, data accuracy, and compliance with Guide requirements across origination, delivery, and servicing.
examples: Post-delivery QC review identifying missing endorsement on title policy
Servicing QC review identifying delinquency misclassification
QC review requiring remediation plan and re-testing
business_rules: QC reviews must be performed at required frequencies and cover required scopes (origination, delivery, servicing).
Findings must be tracked to remediation and validated for closure within timelines.
Repeat findings may trigger escalated oversight, restrictions, or remedies.

GUIDE_BULLETIN.definition: A Freddie Mac-issued bulletin that updates, clarifies, or amends Multifamily Seller/Servicer Guide requirements with stated publication and effective dates.
examples: Bulletin M2026-2 published 04/21/26 affecting multiple chapters
Bulletin updating servicing reporting requirements
Bulletin clarifying delivery documentation expectations
business_rules: Transactions must follow the Guide requirements as amended by applicable Bulletins based on effective dates.
Seller/Servicers must monitor Bulletins and implement changes by required deadlines.
Conflicts between prior guidance and Bulletin updates are resolved per Freddie Mac’s stated applicability rules.

SUBMITS_APPLICATION.definition: The borrower initiates the credit request by providing an application and supporting information for underwriting and decisioning.
examples: Borrower submits a refinance application with sponsorship and property details
Borrower submits a new acquisition application through a Seller/Servicer channel
Borrower submits a revised application after requested corrections
business_rules: Submission completeness requirements determine whether underwriting can proceed and what follow-up documents are needed.
Application data must be traceable to source records used in underwriting and delivery.
Material changes after submission may require re-underwriting or updated third-party reports.

UNDERWRITES.definition: The Seller/Servicer (or its underwriting function) evaluates the application for eligibility, risk, and compliance with program and Guide requirements.
examples: Delegated Seller/Servicer underwrites application using required third-party reports
Non-delegated transaction where Seller/Servicer prepares underwriting package for Freddie Mac review
Underwriting identifies conditions such as insurance endorsements and repair escrows
business_rules: Underwriting must consider required third-party reports and document rationale for key decisions.
Delegated authority limits and approval conditions must be followed and recorded.
Underwriting conclusions must align with delivered loan data and legal documents.

APPROVES_MORTGAGE.definition: The Seller/Servicer issues an approval/commitment for the mortgage consistent with underwriting results and applicable authorities.
examples: Seller/Servicer approves a fixed-rate mortgage subject to final title policy conditions
Approval includes covenants requiring insurance coverage levels and reporting
Approval denied due to unacceptable credit or collateral risk
business_rules: Approval must satisfy delegated authority and Guide eligibility requirements for the selected product.
Approval conditions must be satisfied prior to closing and/or delivery as required.
Denials or adverse terms must be supported by documented reasons and required notices.

SECURES.definition: The property serves as the collateral that secures repayment obligations under the mortgage.
examples: A 200-unit property secures a first-lien Freddie Mac mortgage
Property secures a refinanced mortgage after payoff of prior lien
Property secures a mortgage that is later securitized into a PC pool
business_rules: Collateral eligibility, condition, and legal attributes affect underwriting and required third-party reports.
Collateral must maintain required insurance coverage and title protection through the loan term.
Changes impacting collateral (damage, loss of coverage) require servicing actions and reporting.

EVIDENCES_DEBT.definition: The promissory note evidences the debt obligation that the mortgage represents.
examples: Note executed at closing evidences the mortgage note amount and interest rate
Modified note evidences updated payment terms after a modification
Endorsed note evidences transfer to Freddie Mac at delivery
business_rules: Note terms must match delivered loan data and servicing setup to ensure correct remittance and reporting.
Execution and endorsement requirements must be satisfied for enforceability and delivery acceptance.
Any note modification must follow approval, documentation, and reporting rules.

PERFECTS_LIEN.definition: The security instrument creates and perfects the mortgage lien against the collateral property for the benefit of the mortgagee.
examples: Recorded deed of trust perfects the first-lien position for the mortgage
Security instrument amendment recorded to correct legal description
Security instrument recorded after closing with permitted post-closing delivery
business_rules: Recording must occur in the correct jurisdiction with correct vesting and legal description.
Lien priority and permitted exceptions must align with title insurance coverage.
Post-closing recording delays must be within permitted timelines and controlled by file retention/curative processes.

REQUIRES_APPRAISAL.definition: The mortgage underwriting and delivery process requires an appraisal report to support collateral valuation and loan sizing.
examples: Mortgage requires an appraisal dated within the acceptable recency window at closing
Mortgage requires an appraisal update due to market changes or report staleness
Mortgage requires appraisal review when red flags are identified
business_rules: Appraisal independence, scope, and recency requirements must be met for eligibility.
Appraised value must support LTV and other sizing constraints used in underwriting.
Deficiencies require correction, re-appraisal, or additional review prior to approval/delivery.

REQUIRES_CREDIT_REPORT.definition: The mortgage underwriting process requires a credit report for required parties to assess credit risk and eligibility.
examples: Mortgage requires credit reports for key principals of the borrowing entity
Mortgage requires updated credit report due to elapsed time before closing
Mortgage requires additional credit documentation after derogatory findings
business_rules: Credit report parties, freshness, and content requirements depend on program and transaction structure.
Adverse findings must be evaluated and documented; may affect approval or terms.
Credit report data must reconcile with application disclosures and underwriting narratives.

ISSUES_TITLE_POLICY.definition: The title insurance policy is issued to insure the mortgage lien priority and title marketability subject to allowed exceptions.
examples: Final title policy insures first-lien priority for delivered mortgage
Policy issued with required endorsements and acceptable exceptions
Policy issuance delayed pending curative action for a recorded lien
business_rules: Policy insured party, amount, endorsements, and exceptions must meet delivery requirements.
Unpermitted exceptions must be cured or otherwise resolved before delivery acceptance.
Policy details must align with recorded instrument and closing package.

COVERS_PROPERTY.definition: The hazard insurance policy provides required coverage for the collateral property against specified risks.
examples: Hazard policy covers the property with coverage amount meeting minimum requirements
Renewal policy replaces expiring policy without coverage lapse
Policy endorsement updates loss payee language after servicing transfer
business_rules: Coverage amount and deductible must meet minimum/maximum thresholds throughout the loan term.
Coverage must not lapse; lapses require force-placement or other servicing remedies.
Policy must properly identify insured interests and loss payee/mortgagee requirements.

PRODUCES_CLOSING_PACKAGE.definition: The Seller/Servicer coordinates closing and produces the executed closing package required for delivery and retention.
examples: Closing package assembled with executed note and recorded security instrument
Closing package flagged delivery-ready after final title and insurance evidence received
Closing package returned for correction due to missing signature/acknowledgment
business_rules: Closing package must include all required executed/recorded documents and evidence before final delivery.
Document defects require curative actions within specified timelines.
Closing records must be retained and retrievable under file retention requirements.

DELIVERS_LOAN.definition: The Seller/Servicer submits the mortgage and required data/documents to Freddie Mac as a delivery event for purchase eligibility.
examples: Seller/Servicer delivers a loan after satisfying title insurance conditions
Seller/Servicer delivers loan data first and documents subsequently per permitted process
Delivery resubmitted after correction of a data mismatch
business_rules: Delivery must meet completeness and accuracy standards for both data and documents.
Delivery conditions must be tracked, resolved, and evidenced in the loan file.
Delivery submissions must align with correct Guide version and Bulletin applicability by effective date.

PURCHASES.definition: Freddie Mac reviews a delivery submission and, if acceptable, purchases/settles the delivered mortgage under program terms.
examples: Freddie Mac purchases a delivered mortgage and schedules settlement
Purchase conditioned on curative documentation to be provided post-settlement
Freddie Mac declines purchase due to noncompliance findings
business_rules: Purchase eligibility depends on satisfaction of Guide delivery requirements and conditions.
Settlement terms and pricing (premium/discount) impact accounting net yield setup and ongoing remittance.
Noncompliance may result in purchase denial or post-purchase remedies.

HELD_IN_WAREHOUSE.definition: The mortgage is held under warehousing controls prior to final delivery/purchase, with defined custody and release conditions.
examples: Mortgage held in warehouse pending recording confirmation
Mortgage held in warehouse pending final third-party report update
Mortgage released from warehouse upon Freddie Mac settlement
business_rules: Collateral document custody must be controlled to preserve enforceability and delivery integrity.
Warehousing periods must not cause required reports to become stale for delivery/closing requirements.
Release conditions must align with settlement and custodial transfer requirements.

SERVICES.definition: The Servicer administers the mortgage lifecycle post-closing, including collections, escrow/insurance oversight, default management, and investor reporting.
examples: Servicer collects monthly payments and remits interest and principal per remittance rules
Servicer monitors insurance renewals and enforces coverage requirements
Servicer initiates default management after sustained delinquency
business_rules: Servicing must follow Guide requirements for remittance, reporting, borrower communications, and default actions.
Insurance and title-related obligations continue post-closing and must be monitored and enforced.
Servicing transfers, if allowed, must preserve reporting continuity and compliance.

REPORTS_SERVICING_ACTIVITY.definition: The Servicer submits periodic servicing reports reflecting payment activity, delinquency, and loan status as required by Freddie Mac.
examples: Monthly servicing report submitted for all active loans in portfolio
Servicing report includes 30-day delinquency bucket updates
Corrected servicing report filed after reconciliation
business_rules: Submission frequency, timing, and content must meet specified reporting schedules and formats.
Reported delinquency and status must follow defined calculations and classifications.
Errors and exceptions must be corrected and documented with audit trails.

REPORTS_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD.definition: The Servicer reports interest and yield-related accounting information (including accounting net yield) used to remit and reconcile interest owed to Freddie Mac.
examples: Accounting report shows accounting net yield equal to required net yield for par purchase loans
Accounting report reflects adjusted accounting net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting report flags exception where net yield setup differs from contract
business_rules: Accounting net yield must be reported monthly and calculated per Guide rules, including premium/discount treatment.
Reported yield values must reconcile to remitted interest and servicing system configuration.
Exceptions require timely resolution and may require corrective remittances.

HAS_STATUS.definition: A mortgage is assigned a servicing/accounting status classification (e.g., Active, Delinquent, Deactivated) for reporting and operational actions.
examples: Mortgage transitions from ACTIVE to DELINQUENT after missed payment
Mortgage marked DEACTIVATED for accounting/reporting after servicing action
Mortgage returns to ACTIVE after cure/reinstatement
business_rules: Status transitions must be controlled, date-effective, and consistent with delinquency calculations and Guide definitions.
Deactivation must be justified with a reason and follow specified accounting/reporting rules.
Status determines downstream servicing actions, reporting treatment, and exception handling.

MEASURED_BY_DELINQUENCY.definition: A mortgage’s payment performance is measured by delinquency records indicating days past due and amounts past due as of a given date.
examples: Month-end delinquency record indicates 30 days delinquent
Delinquency record increases to 60 days delinquent after second missed installment
Delinquency record shows cured indicator after reinstatement
business_rules: Delinquency must be computed using consistent rules and applied to reporting and status classification.
Delinquency buckets trigger default management timelines and required actions.
Delinquency records must reconcile with payment posting and suspense application.

SECURITIZES_INTO_POOL.definition: Freddie Mac aggregates eligible mortgages into a mortgage pool for securitization and PC issuance.
examples: Freddie Mac pools eligible delivered mortgages into a 55-Day Multi PC pool
Freddie Mac creates a pool with weighted average coupon and maturity computed from loan data
Pool formed for a specific issuance date and program
business_rules: Only eligible mortgages meeting pooling criteria (product type, status, documentation) may be included in a given pool.
Pool metrics must reconcile to loan-level data used in issuance and investor disclosures.
Pooling events must align with remittance/reporting structures (e.g., payment delay rules).

BACKS_PC.definition: A mortgage pool provides the underlying collateral and cashflow for issuance of a Participation Certificate (PC).
examples: Pool backs a 55-Day Multi PC with 55-day payment delay
Pool backs a PC with defined pass-through rate and distribution rules
Pool backs a PC issuance with multiple underlying mortgages
business_rules: PC terms (delay, pass-through rate) must align with pool characteristics and program rules.
Changes to pool composition must follow controlled processes and eligibility requirements.
Servicer reporting/remittance must support PC investor distribution requirements.

DETERMINES_ACCOUNTING_NET_YIELD.definition: Required Net Yield serves as the baseline reference for Accounting Net Yield, with differences permitted under premium/discount purchase rules.
examples: Required net yield equals accounting net yield when loan is sold at par
Accounting net yield differs from required net yield due to premium purchase
Accounting net yield differs from required net yield due to discount purchase
business_rules: Accounting Net Yield must equal Required Net Yield except for defined premium/discount treatments.
Any divergence must be traceable to purchase pricing and documented calculation rules.
Yield rates must be validated and updated when contract terms or purchase attributes change.

ISSUES_ADVERSE_ACTION_NOTICE.definition: The Seller/Servicer provides an adverse action notice to the borrower when the application for an extension of credit is refused, including required legal information.
examples: Seller/Servicer sends written adverse action notice after denial
Seller/Servicer provides oral notice followed by required written disclosures
Adverse action notice delivered with stated reasons consistent with underwriting file
business_rules: Notice delivery timing and content must comply with applicable law and any Guide-referenced requirements.
Notice reasons must align with underwriting decision documentation.
Notices must be retained per file retention requirements and be available for audit.

RETAINS_RECORDS_UNDER.definition: The Seller/Servicer maintains origination, delivery, and servicing records according to defined retention requirements and accessibility controls.
examples: Seller/Servicer retains closing package documents for the required retention period
Seller/Servicer retains accounting and servicing reports for audit support
Seller/Servicer applies legal hold to a loan file under investigation
business_rules: Retention periods and storage methods must meet Guide and legal requirements for each record type.
Records must remain retrievable and tamper-evident for reviews and audits.
Destruction must be controlled and suspended under legal hold conditions.

REVIEWS_COMPLIANCE.definition: A QC review evaluates closing package completeness and compliance against delivery and documentation standards.
examples: QC review finds missing endorsement on title policy within closing package
QC review identifies signature/acknowledgment defect requiring cure
QC review confirms file completeness and closes with no findings
business_rules: QC must detect missing/defective documents that could impair enforceability or delivery compliance.
Findings must be remediated within required timelines with evidence of cure.
Repeat defects may require process changes and escalated oversight.

UPDATES_GUIDE_REQUIREMENTS.definition: A Guide Bulletin imposes updated or clarified requirements that Seller/Servicers must implement by the stated effective date.
examples: Bulletin M2026-2 requires Seller/Servicers to apply new requirements starting 04/21/26
Bulletin affects servicing reporting processes and system configurations
Bulletin changes delivery documentation expectations for future submissions
business_rules: Seller/Servicers must implement Bulletin changes by effective dates for applicable transactions.
Operational procedures and systems must be updated to reflect amended Guide requirements.
Compliance must be demonstrable through retained evidence, reporting, and QC results.
